title: Calystegia tomentosa
intro: Calystegia tomentosa, also known as Beach Morning Glory or Woolly Bindweed, is a perennial vine native to the coastal regions of North America, characterized by its woody stems and purple flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive problems, due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Calystegia tomentosa include alkaloids such as tropine, scopine, and hyoscyamine, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Calystegia tomentosa is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or dried extract, and is used to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
taxonomy: Calystegia tomentosa, also known as Santa Cruz morning glory, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Convolvulaceae. This classification places it within the order Solanales. Convolvulaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as morning glories. The family is characterized by its twining stems and showy trumpet-shaped flowers. Calystegia tomentosa is one of the several species within the genus Calystegia.
uses: Calystegia tomentosa, also known as the Santa Cruz morning glory, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in alkaloids, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In folk medicine, Calystegia tomentosa is often used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's roots have also been used to treat wounds and skin conditions, due to their antimicrobial properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Calystegia tomentosa.

The plant contains various bioactive compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. preparations: Calystegia tomentosa, also known as Santa Cruz Island morning glory, has been used in herbal preparations for its purported medicinal properties. side_effects: Calystegia tomentosa, also known as Santa Cruz morning glory, is a plant with some potential side effects associated with its use. Ingesting the plant can cause gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. The plant may also induce allergic reactions, including skin irritation and respiratory problems in some individuals. Furthermore, Calystegia tomentosa contains cardenolides, which can be toxic and lead to cardiac issues if ingested in large quantities. It is essential to exercise caution and consult a medical professional before using the plant for any purpose.
